hypothesis: If a classifier-free guided score-based diffusion model is trained only on metastable-state data and its reverse SDE is guided by interpolated conditional scores and then refined with score-aligned ascent using r_SAA = s^B − s^A, then it will generate transition-state configurations with near-0.5 committor and outperform linear/geodesic interpolation baselines across diverse systems because the interpolated score field concentrates probability on the reactive bottleneck and the score difference approximates the reaction coordinate for barrier ascent.
